common.copyFailed: Copy failed, please copy it manually

welcome.title: Set up Vibe Remote
welcome.subtitle: Your local AI coding assistant for Slack, Discord, Telegram, WeChat, and Lark.
welcome.feature1: Runs locally. Your code stays on your computer.
welcome.feature2: SaaS mode uses a cloud relay for Slack delivery, but does not store message content.
welcome.feature3: Self-host mode runs entirely locally via Slack Socket Mode.

modeSelection.title: Choose Mode
modeSelection.saasDescription: OAuth install + cloud relay + local execution (coming soon).
modeSelection.selfHostDescription: Use your own platform credentials and keep execution local.

platform.title: Choose a Platform
platform.subtitle: Select one or more chat platforms to connect.
platform.primaryDesc: Used for compatibility ordering and default UI focus.

slack.title: Slack
slack.desc: Connect a Slack workspace and enable channels.

discord.title: Discord
discord.desc: Connect a Discord bot and choose a guild.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.desc: Connect a Telegram bot for DMs, groups, and forum topics.

lark.title: Lark
lark.desc: Connect to Feishu/Lark workspace for team collaboration

wechat.title: WeChat
wechat.desc: Connect to WeChat personal messaging via iLink bot

discordConfig.title: Discord Configuration
discordConfig.subtitle: Create a Discord app, invite the bot, then add your token.
discordConfig.botTokenHint: Use the token from the Discord Developer Portal.
discordConfig.guildHint: Choose every guild where the bot should respond.
discordConfig.step1Description: Create a new application in the Developer Portal.
discordConfig.step1Item3: Open the Bot tab (your bot is created automatically)
discordConfig.step2Description: Enable Message Content Intent in the Bot tab. Permissions are included in the invite link.
discordConfig.step2Item1: In the Bot tab, enable "Message Content Intent" and save
discordConfig.step2Item2: No need to set Bot Permissions here
discordConfig.step2Tip: Message Content Intent is required to read message text.
discordConfig.step3Description: Paste your Client ID and we will generate a ready-to-use OAuth2 invite link.
discordConfig.step3Item1: In your app, open General Information and copy Client ID
discordConfig.step3Item2: Paste it below to generate the invite URL
discordConfig.step3Item3: Permissions are prefilled in the invite URL
discordConfig.step3Item4: Open the invite URL, choose a server, and authorize
discordConfig.clientIdHint: Find this in General Information of your Discord application.
discordConfig.inviteUrlPlaceholder: Invite URL will appear here after Client ID
discordConfig.step4Description: Copy the bot token and validate access.
discordConfig.step4Item1: In the Bot tab, click "Reset Token" or "View Token"
discordConfig.step4Item2: Copy the token and paste it here
discordConfig.step4Item3: Validate the token and choose a guild
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot1: Make sure your account and the bot are in at least one common server.
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot2: In server Privacy Settings, enable "Allow direct messages from server members".
discordConfig.dmTroubleshoot3: Ensure you didn't block the bot account.

telegramConfig.title: Telegram Configuration
telegramConfig.subtitle: Create a Telegram bot, validate the token, then finish the group setup in a few guided steps.
telegramConfig.step1Description: Use @BotFather to create a new bot and prepare the API token.
telegramConfig.step1Item3: Choose a display name for the bot
telegramConfig.step1Item4: Choose a username ending in "bot" and keep the generated token page open
telegramConfig.step1Tip: If you already created the bot, you can skip straight to copying the token in Step 2.
telegramConfig.step2Description: Copy the bot token from BotFather and verify that Vibe Remote can talk to the bot.
telegramConfig.step2Item1: In BotFather, open your bot and choose the API token option
telegramConfig.step2Item2: Copy the token and paste it below
telegramConfig.step3Description: Set the BotFather switches that directly control whether the bot can join groups and receive the messages you expect.
telegramConfig.step3Item1: Run the commands below in BotFather and select your bot each time
telegramConfig.step3Item2: If you want normal group messages without @mentions, set privacy mode to Disable
telegramConfig.step3Item3: If the bot should be used in groups or forums, make sure group joining is enabled
telegramConfig.step3Item4: Publish the common slash commands so new users can discover them from the Telegram command picker
telegramConfig.step3Command1Description: Send /setprivacy, choose your bot, then select Disable. Otherwise Telegram forwards only commands, @mentions, and replies to the bot.
telegramConfig.step3Command2Title: Allow the bot to join groups
telegramConfig.step3Command2Description: Send /setjoingroups, choose your bot, and make sure it is set to Enable so the bot can be added to groups and forums normally.
telegramConfig.step3Command3Description: Send /setcommands and add common commands like /start, /settings, /new, and /resume so users immediately see what the bot can do.
telegramConfig.step3Tip: If you expect the bot to respond to normal group messages without explicit @mentions, /setprivacy must be set to Disable. This is the most common Telegram setup miss.
telegramConfig.step3ExtraTip: If you only need the bot in DMs for now, /setjoingroups can wait. But before using groups or forum topics, finish it first.
telegramConfig.step4Title: Finish Setup, Bind, Then Discover Chats
telegramConfig.step4Description: Telegram chat discovery happens only after Vibe Remote is running and has received inbound messages. Finish setup first, then come back from the dashboard to enable discovered group or forum chats.
telegramConfig.step4Item1: Finish the setup flow and start the service
telegramConfig.step4Item2: On the summary screen, copy the first bind command and send `bind <code>` or `/bind <code>` to the bot in DM
telegramConfig.step4Item3: After binding, send /start in DM; for groups or forums, add the bot there and grant message permission (plus admin/topic-management rights if you want forum auto-topic mode)
telegramConfig.step4Item4: Send one message in each target group or forum chat, then refresh the Telegram chat list later from the dashboard group settings page
telegramConfig.step4Tip: Direct messages stay hidden from the selectable Telegram chat list. If the bot still reacts only to commands or @mentions in groups, go back to Step 3 and verify that /setprivacy is really set to Disable.
telegramConfig.step5Description: Set the default group reply behavior and forum topic behavior for this bot.
telegramConfig.botTokenHint: Create a bot with @BotFather, then paste the token here.
telegramConfig.requireMention: Require explicit bot targeting in groups
telegramConfig.requireMentionHint: When enabled, the bot responds in groups only when explicitly addressed by command, mention, or reply.
telegramConfig.forumAutoTopicHint: For forum-enabled supergroups, new top-level messages can automatically create a new topic that becomes the session container. This requires the bot to have admin or topic-management rights in that chat.

larkConfig.title: Configure Lark
larkConfig.subtitle: Set up your Lark/Feishu bot in a few steps
larkConfig.domainHint: Select Feishu if your organization uses feishu.cn, or Lark if it uses larksuite.com
larkConfig.step1Description: Create a self-built application on the Open Platform and add the Bot capability.
larkConfig.step1Item1: Go to the Open Platform and click "Create App"
larkConfig.step1Item2: Select "Enterprise Self-Built App" and fill in the app details
larkConfig.step2Description: Enter your App ID and App Secret from the "Credentials & Basic Info" page.
larkConfig.step2WsConnected: Connection established. You can now configure events in the next step.
larkConfig.appIdHint: Found in Credentials & Basic Info page
larkConfig.appSecretHint: Click to reveal and copy from the Open Platform
larkConfig.chatListHint: These are the groups the bot has joined. You can enable specific groups in the next step.
larkConfig.step3Description: In Permission Management, click "Batch Import/Export Permissions" to enable the following permissions.
larkConfig.step3Item4: im:message.group_msg — Receive all group messages (required for non-@mention mode)
larkConfig.step3Item10: im:message:update — Edit/update sent messages and cards
larkConfig.step3Item12: cardkit:card:write — Create and manage card templates
larkConfig.step3Item14: contact:user.base:readonly — Read user basic info (legacy scope)
larkConfig.step3BatchImport: Quick setup: Copy JSON and use "Batch Import/Export Permissions"
larkConfig.step3BatchImportHint: In Permission Management, click "Batch Import/Export Permissions", paste this JSON, and confirm.
larkConfig.step3ManualList: Or manually enable permissions one by one
larkConfig.step4Description: Subscribe to message events and card callbacks, then enable long connection so the bot can receive messages and respond to button interactions in real time.
larkConfig.step4Item2: Set subscription method to "Use Long Connection"
larkConfig.step4Item3: Click "Add Event" and subscribe to "Receive Messages im.message.receive_v1"
larkConfig.step4CallbackDesc: Callbacks handle card button clicks. Without this, menu buttons will not work.
larkConfig.step4CallbackItem2: Click "Add Callback" and add "Card Interaction card.action.trigger"
larkConfig.step4CallbackItem3: Set subscription method to "Use Long Connection" as well
larkConfig.step4Tip: Both events and callbacks must be configured, otherwise the bot cannot receive messages or respond to button actions. Remember to publish a new version after configuration.
larkConfig.step4LongConnFaqDesc: If you only see "Send events to developer server", your app may not have the Bot capability enabled. Go back to Step 1, enter your app → Add Capabilities → enable "Bot". After saving, return to Events & Callbacks and the "Use Long Connection" option will appear.
larkConfig.step4LarkWsWarning: Lark (International) may not have the WebSocket option. If it is unavailable, please use the domestic Feishu version instead.
larkConfig.step5Description: Create and publish a version to make the app available in your organization.
larkConfig.step5Item1: Create a new version and submit for release
larkConfig.step5Tip: After publishing, all configurations (permissions, events, bot capability) will take effect.

wechatConfig.title: WeChat Setup
wechatConfig.subtitle: Scan the QR code with your WeChat to connect.
wechatConfig.startDescription: Click the button below to generate a WeChat login QR code. You will scan it with your phone to connect.
wechatConfig.waitingForScan: Waiting for you to scan the QR code...
wechatConfig.confirmOnPhone: Scanned! Please confirm on your phone.
wechatConfig.scanHint: Open WeChat on your phone and scan this QR code to log in.
wechatConfig.nextStepDesc: You can now send `/start` in WeChat to open the menu.
wechatConfig.qrExpired: QR code expired. Please try again.
wechatConfig.pollError: An error occurred while checking status.
wechatConfig.alreadyBoundDesc: Your WeChat account is already linked. You can proceed or rebind with a different account.

slackConfig.title: Slack Configuration
slackConfig.saasDescription: Authorize the official Slack app and wait for workspace binding.
slackConfig.selfHostDescription: Create a Slack app and configure tokens for Socket Mode.
slackConfig.oauthPrompt: Open OAuth in your browser to authorize Vibe Remote with your Slack workspace.
slackConfig.afterAuth: After authorization, return here to continue.
slackConfig.step1Description: Click the button below to create a new Slack app with pre-configured permissions.
slackConfig.step1Tip: The manifest will be auto-filled. Just select your workspace and click Create.
slackConfig.step2Description: Install the app to your workspace and copy the Bot Token.
slackConfig.step2Item1: In your new app, go to 'OAuth & Permissions'
slackConfig.step3Description: Generate an App-Level Token for Socket Mode connection.
slackConfig.step3Warning: Keep this token secure. It provides full Socket Mode access.
slackConfig.step4Description: Validate your tokens to ensure everything is configured correctly.
slackConfig.step4Item3: The bot will now receive messages from this channel
slackConfig.step4Tip: You can also right-click a channel and select 'Add apps'.

agentDetection.title: Agent Configuration
agentDetection.subtitle: Detect local CLI paths and choose enabled backends.
agentDetection.permissionHint: OpenCode requires permission config to skip approval prompts.
agentDetection.installHint: Agent not found. Click to install automatically.

channelList.title: Group Settings
channelList.subtitle: Enable groups and configure routing for each group.
channelList.inviteBotHint:
Invite the bot to your channel first:
1. Open the channel, click ⚙️ Settings → Integrations → Add apps
2. Or type /invite @YourBotName in the channel
channelList.discordInviteBotHint: Add the bot to your guild and grant access to the channel.
channelList.larkInviteBotHint: Add the bot to a group chat: Open the group → Settings → Bots → Add Bot.
channelList.telegramInviteBotHint: Send a message from the Telegram group or forum to the bot first, then refresh this list.
channelList.wechatInviteBotHint: Send a message to your WeChat bot account to start a conversation.
channelList.telegramDiscoveryInfo: This page shows Telegram groups and forums that the bot has already seen from incoming messages. Direct messages stay hidden here.
channelList.telegramDiscoveryEmptyDesc: Send a message in any Telegram group or forum where the bot is present, then refresh this page.
channelList.accessPolicyHint: Groups are enabled only when checked below.
channelList.noChannelsFound: No groups found. Check token or invite bot.
channelList.addTokenFirst: Add a bot token first to load groups.
channelList.showMessageTypesHint:
Control which message types are displayed in Slack:

• System: Agent startup/status messages
• Assistant: Agent responses and explanations
• Toolcall: Tool execution details (one-line summary)

Note: The agent's final result is always shown regardless of these settings.

summary.title: Review & Finish
summary.subtitle: Confirm settings and start Vibe Remote.
summary.requireMentionHint: Bot only responds when @mentioned in groups
summary.autoUpdateHint: Automatically install updates when system is idle
summary.tipStartCommandDesc: Type /start in any enabled conversation to open the control panel.
summary.tipAgentSwitchDesc: Prefix your message with AgentName: to route to a specific agent. E.g., "Plan: Design the API"
summary.tipThreadDesc: Each thread is an isolated session. Start multiple threads for parallel tasks.
summary.bindCodeDesc: Send this command to the bot in a DM to become the first admin:

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Manage your local Vibe Remote instance.
dashboard.ackModeHint: How bot acknowledges received messages. Falls back to the next mode if the current one is unavailable.
dashboard.errorRetryLimitHint: Auto-retry with 'continue' when LLM stream errors occur (0 = no retry)
dashboard.showDurationHint: Display task elapsed time in result messages
dashboard.includeUserInfoHint: Prepend [username<user_id>] to messages sent to agents
dashboard.replyEnhancementsHint: Enable file attachments and quick-reply buttons in agent responses
dashboard.slackLinkPreviewsHint: Disable Slack URL and media preview cards for messages sent by Vibe Remote

logs.title: System Logs
logs.searchPlaceholder: Search logs by message, logger, or timestamp...

wechat.userBound: Your WeChat account is automatically configured as the bot owner.
wechat.noChannels: WeChat uses direct messaging — no group configuration needed.
wechat.setupComplete: WeChat bot connected! Your account has been automatically configured.

userList.title: Users
userList.subtitle: Manage bound users and their settings
userList.noUsers: No bound users yet. Create a bind code and share it with users.
userList.removeConfirm: Remove this user? This cannot be undone.
userList.lastAdminDemoteWarning: You are removing the last admin role. Continuing will put the bot into an 'unrestricted admin' state, where any bound user can access admin menus. Continue?
userList.lastAdminRemoveWarning: You are removing the last admin user. Continuing will put the bot into an 'unrestricted admin' state, where any bound user can access admin menus. Continue?

bindCode.title: Bind Codes
bindCode.noCodes: No bind codes yet. Create one to get started.
bindCode.usageTitle: How to use a bind code
bindCode.usageHint: Share an active code with the user, then ask them to DM the bot with `bind <code>`. `/bind <code>` still works too.
bindCode.noAvailableCodes: No active bind codes. Create a new code to start binding users.
